items.title: Sunway Construction wins RM1.75bil hyperscale data centre contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Construction Group Bhd has secured a RM1.75 billion contract to build a hyperscale data centre in Bandar Serendah, Selangor, the company said in a bourse filing. The contract, awarded by an international hyperscaler to its unit Sunway Construction Sdn Bhd, covers the construction, completion, testing and commissioning of the facility. Sunway Construction said the project is subject to typical construction risks, particularly fluctuations in material prices, but noted that its experience in large-scale projects should help mitigate these challenges.

items.title: AmBank steps up FDI push into Johor-Singapore SEZ, targets Chinese investors
items.summary: In a recent strategic move, the bank collaborated with the Johor state government to host an investment roundtable in Shanghai, targeting Chinese corporate leaders interested in the region's growing economic potential. Leading the Johor delegation were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, State Investment Committee chairman Lee Ting Han, Invest Johor chief executive Natazha Hariss and Malaysia's consul general in Shanghai, Mohd Shahafeez Shahariz. Lee said that Johor's outreach efforts in Shanghai reflect the increasing importance of Chinese investors in the state's growth story.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan govt halts sand mining in Seremban river
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Negeri Sembilan government has instructed for sand mining activities in Sungai Kampung Jijan here to be stopped following complaints from residents over the activity. State Entrepreneurship, Human Resources, Climate Change, Cooperatives and Consumerism Action Committee chairman S. Veerapan said the state only allows sand mining that adheres to standard operating procedures (SOP). He warned that sand mining permits would be revoked immediately if SOPs are breached.

items.title: MMHE partners South Korea's Hanwha Power on vessel projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Marine and Heavy Engineering Holdings Bhd has teamed up with South Korea's Hanwha Power Systems Co Ltd to collaborate on selected newbuilding and vessel conversion projects. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its unit, Malaysia Marine and Heavy Engineering Sdn Bhd (MMHE), signed a memorandum of understanding with Hanwha Power on April 8, which also covers initiatives aligned with the net-zero agenda. Hanwha Power, part of South Korea's Hanwha Group, will contribute engineering and procurement expertise to support selected newbuilding, as well as conversion and retrofit projects in areas of mutual strategic interest.

items.title: Bus driver, girlfriend get one‑year and six‑month jail terms in viral ‘lap‑driving’ case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — An express bus driver and his girlfriend were sentenced to jail today after pleading guilty in the Magistrates’ Court to dangerous driving last week. Sinar Harian reported that Arif Fahmi Abd Salam, 36, and Nur Adila Najwa Ilham Ameerrullah, 21, entered their pleas before Magistrate Mazana Sinin. Arif Fahmi received a one‑year jail term, while Nur Adila Najwa was sentenced to six months’ imprisonment, with both also fined RM5,000.

items.title: Selangor cops destroy ketum haul worth RM110,000
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police in Selangor have destroyed more than half a tonne of ketum leaves and 2,929 litres of ketum liquid, with an estimated value of RM110,000. State police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the disposal was carried out on Tuesday by the Narcotic Crime Investigation Department (NCID). The cases were linked to several districts, including Shah Alam, Serdang, Sepang, Gombak and Hulu Selangor.

items.title: Malaysia focusing on more resilient and diversified economy: Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is not only focusing on economic growth but also emphasising building a more resilient and diversified economy in the face of global uncertainties, said Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. This follows the World Bank last week raising Malaysia's growth forecast to 4.4 per cent from 4.1 per cent for 2026, despite increasing global uncertainties, including the West Asia conflict. Tengku Zafrul, who is the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) chairman, said the development showed that the country's economy continued to grow but warned that Malaysia should not get too complacent.

items.title: Palm on track for second consecutive weekly loss as crude, soyoil weigh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil futures edged lower on Friday and were poised for a second straight weekly decline, weighed down by weaker crude oil prices and Chicago soyoil. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract rose 0.39 per cent, while its palm oil contract added 1.12 per cent. Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

items.title: 'Jail me': Widower pleads for prison after admitting to cable theft
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 50-year-old widower told the Magistrate's Court here today that he preferred to be sent to prison after pleading guilty to cable theft. Azman Abdullah made the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass after being charged under Section 431A of the Penal Code for committing mischief by damaging cables. The oil palm plantation labourer committed the offence at Kampung Bunagong, Ayer Hitam, at 12.30am on April 15.

items.title: RTD to probe viral bus driver clip, action to follow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) will verify the authenticity and circumstances surrounding a newly surfaced viral video allegedly showing a bus driver behaving in an unsafe manner before determining its next course of action. RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said they would first establish the facts of the case, noting that investigations into such incidents are often conducted in coordination with the police, depending on the elements involved. "If it involves criminal elements, accidents or matters under police jurisdiction, the case will be handled by the police first," he said.

items.title: JPJ probes claims of vehicle number plates being transferred without owners’ knowledge
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has confirmed receiving several complaints regarding alleged transfers of ownership of unique vehicle registration numbers without the original owners’ knowledge. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the department will conduct detailed investigations before taking any action. He said JPJ will carry out investigations and take appropriate action based on existing procedures and laws.

items.title: School dropout arrested over teen's rape and sexual assault
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 17-year-old school dropout has been remanded for seven days to assist in investigations into the alleged rape and sexual assault of a 14-year-old girl he met two days prior. Magistrate Nurasidah A Rahman issued the remand order, which expires next Thursday, following the suspect's arrest yesterday. Both offences carry lengthy jail terms and whipping upon conviction.

items.title: 10 witnesses, fresh evidence as Zara Qairina inquest gathers pace
items.summary: Lawyer Rizwandean M. Borhan said the 70th witness, Assistant Superintendent Mohd Zaidi Abu Hassan, will continue his testimony when proceedings resume next month. The defence is also expected to call expert witnesses, including document examiner Dr Linthini Gannetion, handwriting and document examiners Dr Tay Eue Kam and Dr Dzulkiflee Ismail, and forensic scientist Dr Wan Nur Syuhaila Mat Desa. Additional experts expected to testify include child psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli and psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo.

items.title: Malaysia-India ties built on respect, understanding and friendship, says MP
items.summary: Segamat MP Yuneswaran Ramaraj said this at the 15th anniversary celebration of the Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Indian Cultural Centre (NSCBICC) here recently. Yuneswaran commended the centre for its role in promoting Indian cultural heritage and strengthening people-to-people ties between the two nations over the past 15 years. Yuneswaran said such displays of cultural richness serve as a vital bridge between communities.

items.title: Ericsson slightly lags profit expectations as AI demand drives up chip costs
items.summary: The company reported an adjusted operating profit of 5.2 billion Swedish crowns (US$566 million), excluding restructuring charges, for the first quarter of 2026. Analysts polled by Infront were expecting 5.4 billion crowns on average. The company reported first-quarter net sales of 49.3 billion crowns, compared with an Infront poll estimate of 50.7 billion crowns.

items.title: RTD not ruling out insiders in alleged unauthorised '111' plate transfer
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) is investigating complaints regarding the alleged transfer of unique vehicle registration numbers without the consent of original owners. RTD will look into each case in detail," he said at the launch of the special PP vehicle registration series. Aedy said while the department is committed to assisting those affected, any decision to reverse ownership or take enforcement action would depend on the investigation findings.

items.title: JPJ to review viral video of express bus driver allegedly driving dangerously near KM196.1 southbound
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) will review a recent viral video allegedly showing a bus driver driving dangerously. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said they will need to verify the case first. He said cases involving criminal elements or accidents will first be referred to the police before any further action is taken.

items.title: JPJ to launch ‘P‑P’ special plate series for 80th anniversary, bidding opens April 20 from RM300
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) today announced the launch of a special “P-P” vehicle registration series in conjunction with its 80th anniversary. Bidding for the special “P-P” series is set to open from April 20 to April 24, according to JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Minimum bid prices remain consistent with existing categories, starting from RM300 for running numbers, RM500 for popular numbers, RM2,500 for attractive numbers, RM5,000 for premium numbers, and RM20,000 for golden numbers.

items.title: RTD rolls out 'P-P' plate series to mark 80 years, launches discount sale
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) will introduce a special "P-P" vehicle registration number series to mark its 80th anniversary. Director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the exclusive series symbolises eight decades of the department's role in shaping the country's transport system. Bidding opens on April 20 and closes on April 24 at 10pm, with results to be announced within 24 hours on April 25.

items.title: No room for complacency despite upgraded World Bank economic growth forecast, says Mida chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia is not only focusing on economic growth but also emphasising building a more resilient and diversified economy in the face of global uncertainties, said Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. This follows the World Bank last week raising Malaysia’s growth forecast to 4.4 per cent from 4.1 per cent for 2026, despite increasing global uncertainties, including the West Asia conflict. Tengku Zafrul, who is the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman, said the development showed that the country’s economy continued to grow but warned that Malaysia should not get too complacent.

items.title: Malaysian tanker with Iraqi crude enters Singapore waters after rare Hormuz transit, heads for Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Malaysian crude oil tanker Ocean Thunder entered Singapore waters today as it continued its final approach to Pengerang, Johor. The New Straits Times reported that vessel‑tracking data from the Automatic Identification System showed the Panama‑flagged very large crude carrier moving at 7.1 knots on a 66‑degree course through the Singapore Strait. The 274‑metre tanker, drawing 17 metres, is carrying about one million barrels of Iraqi Basrah Heavy crude bound for the Pengerang Integrated Petroleum Complex operated by Petronas.

items.title: Govt farming scheme earns RM22m, helps over 2,300 Malaysians earn extra income
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The People’s Income Initiative-Agricultural Entrepreneurship (IPR-Intan) programme under the Ministry of Economy has generated cumulative revenue of about RM22 million since its introduction in 2023, said Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. “One of the (IPR programme) segments is Intan, which focuses on agriculture, and so far it has produced about 2,300 participants with cumulative revenue reaching RM22 million,” he told reporters after visiting the IPR-Intan project site here today. Meanwhile, in Putrajaya, Akmal Nasrullah said the chilli cultivation project using fertigation on 4.04 hectares has benefited 20 participants, each earning an average monthly income of about RM3,000.

items.title: Hospital accused of 'license abuse', cover-up as staff cancer cluster emerges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Allegations of negligence and a systemic cover-up on radiation safety at a hospital have sparked outrage, with a former staff member claiming safety lapses have created a "cancer cluster" among personnel. The former staff, identified only as Fauziah, is a former radiographer at the hospital's Nuclear Medicine Unit who is now battling Stage 4 Hodgkin Lymphoma. At a Press conference today organised by #ReformIPT, she shared evidence on the alleged safety lapses at the hospital.

items.title: Jail and RM5k fine for bus driver, girlfriend in reckless driving case
items.summary: JASIN: A bus driver caught on camera allowing his girlfriend to sit on his lap while driving has been sentenced to one year in jail for reckless driving. Arif Fahmi Abd Salam, 36, was also fined RM5,000 and disqualified from holding or obtaining a driving licence for five years. She was also disqualified from holding a driving licence for five years.

items.title: Malaysia’s inflation remains modest but ticks up in March 2026, govt data shows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia’s inflation increased modestly to 1.7 per cent in March 2026, with the consumer price index (CPI) climbing to 136.4 from 134.1 a year earlier, the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said. In a statement today, chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said Malaysia’s inflation in March 2026 was mainly driven by an increase in the transport group to 1.6 per cent from -0.7 per cent in February 2026. Inflation was also driven by increases in the personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services group, which rose by seven per cent (February 2026: 6.9 per cent).

items.title: Activists mulling legal action against hospital for alleged radiation exposure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A hospital in the Klang Valley is facing potential legal action over alleged negligence in radiation safety protocols , which is alleged to have put both healthcare workers and patients at grave risk. During a press briefing today, a representative from AIMI & EZ Advocates & Solicitors, said while radiation exposure is an inherent risk in nuclear medicine, it must be managed as a "controlled risk". Drawing an analogy to a construction site, the representative Siti Nur Sabrina Mohammad said just as employers must mandate safety helmets, the hospital has a legal duty to provide continuous monitoring.

items.title: Inland fisheries drive Perak economy as state leads Malaysia in production share
items.summary: IPOH, April 17 — Perak recorded the highest inland fisheries landings in the country in 2024 at 2,987 metric tonnes, accounting for 35 per cent of Malaysia’s total landings of 9,652 metric tonnes. State Rural Development, Plantation, Agriculture and Food Industry Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Zolkafly Harun said the achievement reflected the state government’s continued commitment to supporting and developing the inland fisheries sector. He said a total of 1,893 licensed inland fishermen were registered with the Perak Fisheries Department last year.

items.title: Trump characterises Iran war as a 'little diversion'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Thursday the US war with Iran was a "little diversion" during his second term in power, as recent polling shows the conflict's unpopularity with the American public. An Ipsos poll conducted last weekend found 51 per cent of the more than 1,000 respondents thought the Iran war was not worth the costs associated with it. Another recent poll published Wednesday by Quinnipiac University found 65 per cent of US voters blame Trump for the recent rise in gas prices prompted by the Strait of Hormuz closing since the start of the Iran war.

items.title: MPOC secures RM47.5m potential palm oil sales from Middle East trade mission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Palm Oil Council (MPOC) has strengthened Malaysia's push into emerging markets following the successful conclusion of a trade networking visit involving 35 buyers from Central Asian Republics. A key highlight of the programme was the BizMatch session, which facilitated 352 structured business meetings between 18 Malaysian suppliers and the visiting buyers. "The strong engagement observed during this visit reflects increasing confidence among buyers and the readiness of Malaysian suppliers to support this market," she added.

items.title: Malaysia’s economy set to grow 5.3pc in Q1 2026, DOSM says in advance estimate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia’s economy is projected to grow by 5.3 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026) based on advance estimates, reflecting an economy that remains fundamentally resilient, despite rising global uncertainties, particularly elevated oil prices driven by geopolitical tensions. said the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). Its chief statistician, Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin, said the manufacturing sector maintained positive momentum in the first two months of 2026, with output expanding by 7.3 per cent and 4.2 per cent, respectively, largely driven by export-oriented industries.

items.title: Malaysia inflation rises 1.7pct in March on higher transport and services costs
items.summary: Across the groups, the insurance and financial services sector rose to 4.9 per cent from February, alcoholic beverages and tobacco climbed to 2.7 per cent, restaurant and accommodation services inched higher to 2.6 per cent, health climbed to 1.4 per cent, and information and communication rose to 1.4 per cent. The monthly headline inflation in March increased to 0.3 per cent as compared to 0.2 per cent in February, while for the first quarter, inflation rose to 1.6 per cent compared to 1.3 per cent in the fourth quarter of 2025. At the state level, eight states recorded increases above the national inflation level (1.7 per cent), namely Pahang (2.5 per cent), Labuan (2.4 per cent), Negeri Sembilan (2.2 per cent), Kuala Lumpur (2.2 per cent), Sabah (2.1 per cent), Johor (2.0 per cent), Kedah (1.9 per cent) and Penang (1.8 per cent).

items.title: LNG prices to track oil price trends in Asia amid ongoing diplomacy: Rystad Energy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global liquefied natural gas (LNG) price movements, especially in Asia, are expected to continue tracking oil price trends, as oil fuel switching remains prevalent in more price-sensitive countries, according to Norway-based energy research company Rystad Energy. Its research analyst Mathieu Utting said in a note that despite the United States and Iran walking away from talks in Pakistan last weekend without reaching a peace agreement, ongoing diplomatic discussions between both parties have helped calm global LNG prices. "The ongoing diplomatic discussions between both have calmed global LNG prices, with Asia LNG derivatives price for June having decreased 14 per cent week-over-week to US$16.50 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) this week.

items.title: April 30 set for decision on preacher Ebit Lew’s 11 sexual harassment charges
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 17 — The Magistrates’ Court in Tenom has fixed April 30 for its decision on the prosecution case against preacher Ebit Irawan Lew, who faces 11 charges of sexual harassment. Deputy public prosecutors Nor Azizah Mohamad, Analia Kamaruddin and Muhammad Hidayat Wahab acted for the prosecution, while Lew is represented by Ram Singh, alongside Kamarudin Mohmad Chinki and Prabjit Singh Gill. — Bernama

items.title: Vale appoints Edwin Gerard as CEO of Malaysia unit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global mining giant Vale S.A. has appointed Edwin Gerard as chief executive officer of its Malaysian subsidiary, Vale Malaysia Minerals Sdn Bhd, marking a leadership transition as the group strengthens its long-term strategic footprint in the country. Since commencing operations in Teluk Rubiah in 2014, Vale has invested approximately RM80 million in social development initiatives, reinforcing its long-standing commitment to community development alongside industrial growth. The facility serves as a critical distribution centre for Vale's Asia-Pacific operations and remains central to the group's focus on safe, reliable and sustainable operations.

items.title: Contractor charged with using forged documents to secure govt contracts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A contractor has pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court to six counts of using forged documents to secure government tenders. Mohd Safri Ibrahim, 47, claimed trial to all charges read before judge Nor Aziati Jaafar. Deputy public prosecutor Syairah Aqilah Khalil prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Ahmad Hafiz A. Bakar.

items.title: Abandoned toddler: authorities race to trace missing mother
items.summary: In a statement, the Social Welfare Department (JKM) of Seberang Perai Utara (SPU) said the child, identified as Amanda Mikayla Abdullah, was allegedly left by her mother with a caretaker, Layla Yamna Abdul Hajar, without any identification documents. "Investigations by the Child Protector of the SPU District JKM found that the child's biological mother, Nurul Izzati Mohamad A'sri, 29, cannot be contacted and has yet to be located. Members of the public with information on the child's family are urged to contact SPU District Child Protector Norpatin Bakar at 04-5758715 or 04-5758716, or via email at norpatin@jkm.gov.my.

items.title: Dead fish found at Sungai Ketil raise pollution fears
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Hundreds of dead fish found at Taman Rimba Sungai Ketil this morning have sparked health and safety concerns among visitors at the popular recreational spot. Angler Muhammad Che Mahmood, 29, found carcasses scattered along the riverbank about 7.45am. Muhammad said he arrived early to fish but found the area deserted and the water unusually murky and dark, which he initially attributed to heavy rain the previous night.

items.title: Dashcam video captures moment white Alza swerves into opposite lane before head‑on crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — A viral dashcam recording showing a car veering into oncoming traffic before a head‑on collision has drawn wide attention online. The video shows the vehicle swerving abruptly before crossing into the opposite lane and crashing into an oncoming Honda HR‑V and the dashcam vehicle. The video shows the vehicle swerving abruptly before crossing into the opposite lane and crashing into an oncoming Honda HR‑V and the dashcam vehicle.

items.title: Malaysian vessel Ocean Thunder enters Singapore waters after return from Iran
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Malaysian crude oil tanker Ocean Thunder has entered Singapore waters and is on its final approach to Pengerang, with vessel tracking data indicating an arrival time of 1.30pm today. Automatic Identification System (AIS) updates show the Panama-flagged very large crude carrier (VLCC) is underway at 7.1 knots on a steady course of 66 degrees, threading through the Singapore Strait. The 274-metre vessel, drawing 17 metres, is carrying approximately one million barrels of Iraqi Basrah Heavy crude destined for the Pengerang Integrated Petroleum Complex (PIPC), a downstream hub operated by Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).

items.title: Economy minister: Govt to raise biodiesel blend gradually from B10 to B15 to avoid industry disruption
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The implementation of the B15 biodiesel blend rate will be carried out in stages to avoid disruption to the existing operations of the industry, said Minister of Economy Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the progressive approach is important as operational aspects, particularly at blending centres, need to be organised meticulously without disrupting the current supply chain. B10 refers to 10 per cent biodiesel and 90 per cent regular diesel.

items.title: Mother, daughter among three charged over robbery, assault in Penang
items.summary: GEORGETOWN: A mother and daughter were among three individuals charged in a Sessions Court here today with robbery and causing injury to a man. The accused – G Thananjayan, 41; Nur Fadzlin Mohamed Salim, 27; and her mother Azura Abdul Latiff, 42 – pleaded not guilty after the charges were read before Judge Juraidah Abbas. The prosecution proposed bail at RM8,000 for each accused.

items.title: Perak faces school capacity pressure with nearly 30,000 Year One applications for 2027
items.summary: State Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Khairudin Abu Hanipah said 29,610 applications had been received as of April 7, with more than 25,000 involving children aged six and above. He said district education offices are working with schools to assess readiness and identify capacity gaps ahead of the intake. Khairudin said the state has outlined several measures to manage the expected surge, including optimising 411 existing schools under the Education Ministry.

items.title: Malaysia economy grows 5.3pct in Q1 2026, underpinned by resilient manufacturing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy expanded by 5.3 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, according to advance estimates released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), moderating from the 6.3 per cent growth recorded in the previous quarter. The manufacturing sector sustained positive momentum in the first two months of 2026, with output expanding by 7.3 per cent and 4.2 per cent, respectively, driven largely by export-oriented industries. The services sector continued to anchor overall expansion, registering a 5.4 per cent growth attributed to the growth mainly in the wholesale and retail trade sub-sector.

items.title: Energy crisis demands focus on food security, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The global energy crisis requires the government to prioritise food security, says Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the primary challenge currently facing the plantation and agriculture sectors is the rising cost of fertiliser, which has driven up overall production costs. "One example is fuel, specifically diesel, and the second is fertiliser.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in seven states until 1pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in seven states until 1pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the states expected to be affected include Perak (Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak), Pahang (Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Klang, Kuala Langat and Sepang), and Negeri Sembilan (Port Dickson). Also affected are Melaka, Johor (Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat and Pontian) and Sarawak, involving Kuching, Samarahan and Sri Aman.

items.title: Selangor Immigration busts Bukit Rimau massage centres using WhatsApp for sex‑service bookings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Selangor Immigration officers raided several massage centres in Bukit Rimau that allegedly offered illicit services through WhatsApp bookings supported by photo catalogues of women. The operation, conducted under Op Gegar at 8pm yesterday, involved 37 enforcement officers targeting premises suspected of running prostitution activities, according to a report by Berita Harian . Selangor Immigration director Khairrul Aminus Kamaruddin said the syndicate used online bookings, adding that “customers selected women based on photos sent through WhatsApp before coming to the premises for massage packages and sexual services.” He said officers detained 11 foreign nationals aged between 18 and 40, comprising one Vietnamese woman, four Thai women, two Chinese nationals and four Myanmar nationals.

items.title: Man, 36, to face charge after Wakaf Bharu brawl caught in viral clip, says Kelantan police
items.summary: Tumpat District Police Chief ACP Mohd Khairi Shafie said that following the incident, a 36-year-old male suspect will be charged at the Tumpat Magistrate’s Court on April 19. Elaborating further, Mohd Khairi said police received a report on the incident on April 1 at 12.45 am, and the case was also classified under Section 147 of the Penal Code. Mohd Khairi said a total of 11 individuals, aged between 18 and 67, were arrested to assist in investigations.

items.title: Safety over shortcuts: Kelantan border community turn to Rantau Panjang ICQS after action over illegal Sg Golok crossings
items.summary: PASIR MAS, April 17 — A directive by Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail for assistance to be given to 34 Thai students caught crossing the Malaysia-Thai border illegally last Sunday comes amid growing awareness of the dangers associated with unlawful routes along Sungai Golok. More residents, particularly students and parents, are now opting for legal entry and exit routes between the two countries, prioritising safety over convenience. Deputy Commander of the Rantau Panjang Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), Bakri Sulaiman, said choosing legal routes not only ensures compliance with the law but also safeguards lives.

items.title: Good, but needs fixing: Bintulu DAP leader welcomes new Summerville Villa park but flags maintenance issues
items.summary: BINTULU, April 17 — The newly completed recreational park at Summerville Villa here has been welcomed as a positive addition to the neighbourhood, offering residents a dedicated space for leisure, exercise and community interaction. However, several maintenance issues identified shortly after completion need to be addressed promptly to ensure the facility remains safe, practical and comfortable for public use. Democratic Action Party (DAP) Bintulu chairman Chiew Chan Yew said among the concerns observed during a recent site inspection were water ponding at both entrances during rainy weather, a damaged drain cover, worn-out benches and landscaping issues involving trees affected by parasitic plants.

items.title: ‘Grab the chance’: Bukit Assek rep urges SPM leavers to use Sarawak’s free tertiary education scheme
items.summary: SIBU, April 17 — Bukit Assek assemblyman Joseph Chieng Jin Ek has urged SPM school leavers to seize upcoming state-backed education opportunities, including Sarawak’s free tertiary education scheme (FTES), as they prepare for the next phase of their academic journey. “This is a major opportunity for our students to further their studies without the burden of fees,” Chieng said during the SMK Bukit Assek 2025 SPM Excellence Incentive Award Ceremony. Every success carries meaning,” he said.

items.title: Adly Zahari: Over RM300m allocated for veterans’ welfare as Mindef expands outreach in rural Sarawak
items.summary: DARO, April 17 — More than RM300 million has been allocated this year to enhance the welfare, healthcare, housing and socio-economic well-being of Armed Forces veterans nationwide, as the Ministry of Defence steps up outreach efforts in rural Sarawak. The outreach programme, organised by the ministry through the Department of Veterans Affairs (JHEV ATM), also saw the distribution of welfare aid including basic necessities, medical equipment, home repair assistance and 100 Rahmah baskets to local veterans’ associations. He said the ministry continues to prioritise key areas affecting veterans, including healthcare, housing and income sustainability.

items.title: Fuel lorries on Labuan ferries prompts safety review on Menumbok–Labuan service
items.summary: LABUAN, April 17 — Lorries transporting gas and petroleum products for petrol stations aboard Ro-Ro ferries plying the Menumbok and Labuan route have been identified as posing safety risks to commuters and vehicle owners. Labuan Marine Department director Alimuddin Amirudin said the department is responsible for ensuring the safety of all onboard, particularly when hazardous and flammable cargo is involved. There are currently six ferries operating along the Menumbok-Labuan-Menumbok route, namely MV Goodwill Star, MV Putrajaya 1, MV Kimanis 1, MV Joy Star, MV Blue Ocean and MV Galaxy, facilitating the movement of vehicles, passengers and goods to the island daily.

items.title: Former bus driver, girlfriend to be charged over viral ‘lap‑driving’ video on PLUS highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — A former express bus driver and his girlfriend will be charged in the Jasin Magistrate’s Court this morning over a viral video showing him driving while holding her on his lap. Berita Harian reported that police confirmed both suspects would be brought to court today following the circulation of the eight‑second clip on social media. Jasin district police chief Superintendent Lee Robert said the 36‑year‑old man and his 21‑year‑old girlfriend would face charges based on the findings of the investigation, adding that “the Deputy Public Prosecutor has given instructions for prosecution and both suspects will be brought to the Jasin Magistrate’s Court.” He said the charges follow information gathered by investigators after the video drew public attention earlier this week.

items.title: Police begin surveillance at 13 petrol stations in Perak, Kedah and Perlis along Malaysia–Thailand border
items.summary: IPOH, April 17 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) through its Internal Security and Public Order Department (JKDNKA) has tightened its control at 13 fuel stations identified as ‘hotspots’ in Perak, Kedah and Perlis under Op Tiris 4.0. The General Operations Force (GOF) Northern Brigade Commander SAC Balveer Singh Mahindar Singh said three senior police officers and 54 personnel from the brigade have been deployed to the locations along the Malaysia-Thailand border, comprising two in Perak, six in Kedah and five in Perlis. He said the move is aimed at curbing the supply leakage of subsidised fuel at the targeted locations.

items.title: Malay Chambers backs planned AI-based labour recruitment system to boost integrity, cut out illegal agents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The implementation of a more transparent foreign worker recruitment system can help close the space for unethical practices, thereby increasing industry confidence in existing mechanisms. Norsyahrin, who fully supports the efforts by the Ministry of Human Resources to strengthen the foreign worker recruitment system, said it not only addresses industry issues that require foreign labour but also helps streamline the overall recruitment process from source countries. Previously, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said his ministry plans to introduce a recruitment system based on technology and artificial intelligence (AI).

items.title: MOH to introduce new prescription format with fuller patient and drug details from 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Health Ministry will introduce a new prescription format that requires more detailed patient information, including allergy status and full medication specifications. Current prescription format Requires only basic patient information. New prescription format Requires full patient identification and clinical details.

items.title: More listings, bigger firms: Govt eyes 10 IPOs and RM500m Bumiputera champions within five years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Madani government remains committed to supporting and energising the agenda to empower Bumiputera entrepreneurs as productive, achievement-driven contributors, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said that by strengthening the Bumiputera Commercial and Industrial Community (BCIC), the government has set a bolder target, raising investment by government-linked investment companies (GLICs) to as much as RM2 billion. He added that the plan also includes achieving at least 10 new public listings and nurturing Bumiputera champions capable of building companies valued at RM500 million within five years.

items.title: Too hot, too many fires: Bachok tops list as Kelantan battles spike in open burning cases
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 17 — The Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has sounded the alarm over a surge in open burning, having responded to 1,509 emergency calls since March amid a heatwave that continues to grip the state. The Kelantan JBPM Operations Centre, in a statement yesterday, said Bachok district recorded the highest number of incidents with 287 cases, followed by Kota Bharu (193), Pasir Mas (192), and Ketereh (127). “JBPM is currently leading an integrated operation with the Department of Environment (DOE), the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), and the Civil Defence Force (APM) to extinguish a massive 30-hectare peatland fire in Kampung Kual, Bachok,” the statement said.

items.title: TikTok video of handcuffed woman at ‘Merlimau’ station’ sparks investigation, police say likely AI-generated
items.summary: District police chief Supt Lee Robert said the content showed two policemen escorting a woman, believed to be handcuffed behind her back, outside a building bearing the signage ‘Merlimau Police Station’. The investigation was initiated following a report lodged by a police officer from the police station at 1.04 pm yesterday. Lee said the report was lodged as the content could potentially tarnish the image of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) as well as the police station concerned.

items.title: RM50 start, RM1 million finish: Habitual saver lands BSN SSP dream after 12 years
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 17 — After more than a decade of saving, a state administrative officer has finally realised his dream of becoming a millionaire after he was announced as the winner of the 122nd Bank Simpanan Nasional Premium Savings Certificate (BSN SSP) Millionaire draw. Not long after, I received a call from BSN informing me that I was the winner of the March SSP draw. He said the first thing he did was call his wife, Zarifah Zulkepli, 33, to share the news.
